title: Dactylorhiza angustata
intro: Dactylorhiza angustata, also known as the narrow-leaved orchid, is a type of terrestrial orchid belonging to the genus Dactylorhiza. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Dactylorhiza angustata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tincture, decoction, or powder, often in combination with other herbs, to support respiratory health and immunity.
taxonomy: Dactylorhiza angustata is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Dactylorhiza and is further classified under the subgenus Dactylorhiza. The species is native to parts of Asia. Dactylorhiza angustata is closely related to other species within the Dactylorhiza genus, which are also commonly known as spotted orchids. The classification of Dactylorhiza angustata is a subject of ongoing research and study in the field of botany.
uses: Dactylorhiza angustata has been used in traditional medicine, although its effectiveness and safety have not been extensively scientifically proven. The plant's extracts and rhizomes are reported to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. In traditional practices, it has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. Some studies suggest that the plant's extracts may have antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects. However, more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal potential and to standardize its use in modern medicine.

These compounds have been found to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The iridoid glycosides present in Dactylorhiza angustata are also known to have a potential effect on blood sugar levels and lipid metabolism.
